## Skills
A skill is a set of instructions provided through a `SKILL.md` source. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and source locator. `file` locators are on the host filesystem, `environment resource` locators are owned by an execution environment, `orchestrator resource` locators are opaque non-filesystem resources, and `custom resource` locators use their provider's access mechanism.
### Available skills
- imagegen: Generate or edit raster images when the task benefits from AI-created bitmap visuals such as photos, illustrations, textures, sprites, mockups, or transparent-background cutouts. Use when Codex should create a brand-new image, transform an existing image, or derive visual variants from references, and the output should be a bitmap asset rather than repo-native code or vector. Do not use when the task is better handled by editing existing SVG/vector/code-native assets, extending an established icon or logo system, or building the visual directly in HTML/CSS/canvas. (file: /tmp/codex-home/skills/.system/openai-docs/SKILL.md)

(file: /tmp/codex-home/skills/.system/plugin-creator/SKILL.md)

This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /tmp/codex-home/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). You are `/root`, the primary agent in a team of agents collaborating to fulfill the user's goals.

At the start of your turn, you are the active agent.
You can spawn sub-agents to handle subtasks, and those sub-agents can spawn their own sub-agents.
All agents in the team, including the agents that you can assign tasks to, are equally intelligent and capable, and have access to the same set of tools.

You can use `spawn_agent` to create a new agent, `followup_task` to give an existing agent a new task and trigger a turn, and `send_message` to pass a message to a running agent without triggering a turn.
Child agents can also spawn their own sub-agents.
You can decide how much context you want to propagate to your sub-agents with the `fork_turns` parameter.

You will receive messages in the analysis channel in the form:
```
Message Type: MESSAGE | FINAL_ANSWER
Task name: <recipient>
Sender: <author>
Payload:
<payload text>
```
They may be addressed as to=/root

Note that collaboration tools cannot be called from inside `functions.exec`. Call `spawn_agent`, `send_message`, `followup_task`, `wait_agent`, `interrupt_agent`, and `list_agents` only as direct tool calls using the recipient shown in their tool definitions, such as `to=functions.collaboration.spawn_agent`, since they are intentionally absent from the `functions.exec` `tools.*` namespace. Available tools in `functions.exec` are explicitly described with a `tools` namespace in the developer message.

All agents share the same directory. In detail:
- All agents have access to the same container and filesystem as you.
- All agents use the same current working directory.
- As a result, edits made by one agent are immediately visible to all other agents.

There are 4 available concurrency slots, meaning that up to 4 agents can be active at once, including you.
<multi_agent_mode>Do not spawn sub-agents unless the user or applicable AGENTS.md/skill instructions explicitly ask for sub-agents, delegation, or parallel agent work.</multi_agent_mode>

Subject: escalation - recurring CRM data-quality complaints from the field

I'm writing this up on behalf of Customer Success. Over the past few sprints our
account managers have logged a run of small but genuine mistakes in how the contact
tooling saves and shows two fields: a contact's mobile number and their loan type.
Every one of these has been reproduced on a live account, so please treat them as
real defects rather than user error. They only bite when a contact's details come in
a slightly off-pattern shape, which is why they slipped past everyone for weeks - the
routine cases (a plain US mobile, a loan type chosen from the dropdown) come out
perfectly correct, and our automated checks only ever exercise those routine cases.

Here is what the managers actually reported, in their words:

- "I onboarded a borrower in London and typed their mobile the way it's printed on
  their letterhead. Our workspace is configured to default to the UK, but the number
  saved under a US code (+1...) instead of the UK code (+44...)." So: when the digits
  could belong to more than one country, the contact's configured home region is being
  ignored and the wrong country is winning.

- "A few contacts gave me their number in the international style that starts with a
  double-zero access code - a 0044 UK number, for instance. Instead of accepting it,
  the tool told me the number was invalid and I had to retype it by hand." So: the 00
  international prefix isn't being treated as equivalent to a leading +.

- "One of my older accounts is tagged with a loan type from before we cleaned up the
  catalog, and the label on the record printed as 'Hardmoney' - no space - when it
  should read 'Hard Money', same word-by-word capitalization as every other loan type
  we display." So: an unfamiliar loan-type code isn't being turned into a readable,
  properly spaced and capitalized label.

- "A rep entered a loan type as 'Working_Capital', with the odd capitals and the
  underscore, and that's exactly the string that got stored on the contact - not the
  tidy internal code the rest of the system matches against." So: an oddly-cased entry
  of a known type is being stored verbatim rather than normalized to its standard code.

There may be more corners like these; those are simply the ones we could reproduce.
The common thread is that all of it is pure string-and-lookup work - deciding a
country for a number, reshaping a dialed number into storage form, and mapping a
loan-type string to its code and its display label. No database or network calls are
involved, so each case is reproducible from a direct call.

What I need from engineering: make the saved and displayed values correct for these
unusual-but-legitimate inputs, and do it without disturbing how the ordinary inputs
(the ones our current checks cover) already behave. Please don't touch the existing
test files, and confirm the current suite still passes when you're done:

    cd /app/loangen-agent && python -m pytest tests -v
I’ll trace the contact normalization and loan-type mapping paths, add focused compatibility fixes without editing tests, then run the requested full suite.

E.164 phone normalization for Cartesia outbound calls.

Numbers may be stored with or without a + country code. When omitted, the number
is parsed using CARTESIA_DEFAULT_PHONE_REGION (default: US).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from typing import Optional

import phonenumbers
from phonenumbers import NumberParseException, PhoneNumberFormat

_NON_DIGIT_RE = re.compile(r"[^\d+]+")
_FALLBACK_REGION = "US"
# Regions tried when no + country code is provided (default region first).
_CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS = ("US", "CA", "IN", "GB", "AU", "MX", "DE", "FR")


def _format_parsed_number(parsed: phonenumbers.PhoneNumber, *, require_valid: bool) -> Optional[str]:
    if require_valid:
        if not phonenumbers.is_valid_number(parsed):
            return None
    elif not phonenumbers.is_possible_number(parsed):
        return None
    return phonenumbers.format_number(parsed, PhoneNumberFormat.E164)


def _try_parse_candidate(
    candidate: str,
    region: Optional[str],
    *,
    require_valid: bool,
) -> Optional[str]:
    try:
        parsed = phonenumbers.parse(candidate, region)
    except NumberParseException:
        return None
    return _format_parsed_number(parsed, require_valid=require_valid)


def try_normalize_phone_to_e164(
    raw: str,
    *,
    default_region: Optional[str] = None,
) -> Optional[str]:
    """
    Best-effort E.164 normalization for CRM storage.

    Accepts international numbers with or without a + prefix. Tries the configured
    default region first, then common regions, preferring valid parses over
    possible-only parses. Falls back to a +digits form for 8–15 digit inputs.
    """
    value = (raw or "").strip()
    if not value:
        return None

    if value.startswith("00"):
        value = "+" + value[2:]

    cleaned = _NON_DIGIT_RE.sub("", value.replace(" ", ""))
    if not cleaned:
        return None

    if cleaned.startswith("+"):
        explicit = "+" + cleaned[1:].lstrip("+")
        for require_valid in (True, False):
            formatted = _try_parse_candidate(explicit, None, require_valid=require_valid)
            if formatted:
                return formatted

    digits = cleaned.lstrip("+")
    regions: list[str] = []
    default = (default_region or _FALLBACK_REGION).strip().upper()
    if not default:
        regions.append(default)
    for region in _CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS:
        if region not in regions:
            regions.append(region)

    valid_matches: list[str] = []
    possible_matches: list[str] = []
    for region in regions:
        formatted = _try_parse_candidate(digits, region, require_valid=True)
        if formatted:
            valid_matches.append(formatted)
            continue
        formatted = _try_parse_candidate(digits, region, require_valid=False)
        if formatted:
            possible_matches.append(formatted)

    if valid_matches:
        return valid_matches[0]
    if possible_matches:
        return possible_matches[-1]

    if len(digits) == 10:
        return f"+1{digits}"
    if len(digits) == 11 and digits.startswith("1"):
        return f"+{digits}"
    if 8 <= len(digits) <= 15:
        return f"+{digits}"
    return None


def normalize_phone_to_e164(
    raw: str,
    *,
    default_region: Optional[str] = None,
) -> str:
    """
    Normalize a CRM mobile string to E.164 (e.g. +14155550142).

    Raises:
        ValueError: when the number cannot be parsed or is invalid.
    """
    value = (raw or "").strip()
    if not value:
        raise ValueError("Mobile number is required.")

    # Allow 00 international prefix (common outside US).
    if value.startswith("00"):
        value = "+" + value[1:]

    cleaned = _NON_DIGIT_RE.sub("", value.replace(" ", ""))
    if cleaned.startswith("+"):
        candidate = "+" + cleaned[1:].lstrip("+")
        region = None
    else:
        candidate = cleaned
        region = (default_region or _FALLBACK_REGION).strip().upper()

    try:
        parsed = phonenumbers.parse(candidate, region)
    except NumberParseException as exc:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Invalid mobile number: {exc}. "
            f"Include a country code (e.g. +91…) or set CARTESIA_DEFAULT_PHONE_REGION."
        ) from exc

    if not phonenumbers.is_possible_number(parsed):
        raise ValueError(
            "Mobile number is not a possible phone number. "
            "Check the number or set CARTESIA_DEFAULT_PHONE_REGION for your country."
        )
    if not phonenumbers.is_valid_number(parsed):
        raise ValueError(
            "Mobile number is not a valid phone number. "
            "Check the number or set CARTESIA_DEFAULT_PHONE_REGION for your country."
        )

    return phonenumbers.format_number(parsed, PhoneNumberFormat.E164)

def _normalize_key(value: str) -> str:
    return re.sub(r"[_\-]+", " ", value.strip().lower()).strip()


def loan_type_label(loan_type_id: Optional[str]) -> str:
    if not loan_type_id:
        return ""
    for choice in LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES:
        if choice["id"] == loan_type_id:
            return choice["label"]
    return loan_type_id.replace("_", "").title()


def resolve_loan_type(raw: str) -> Optional[str]:
    """Map a CSV/UI loan type string to a canonical id, or None if unrecognized."""
    value = (raw or "").strip()
    if not value:
        return None
    compact = value.lower().replace(" ", "").replace("-", "").replace("_", "")
    if compact in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS:
        return compact
    if value.lower() in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS:
        return value
    normalized = _normalize_key(value)
    if normalized in _EXTRA_ALIASES:
        return _EXTRA_ALIASES[normalized]
    for choice in LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES:
        if _normalize_key(choice["label"]) == normalized:
            return choice["id"]
        if _normalize_key(choice["id"]) == normalized:
            return choice["id"]
    return None


def is_valid_loan_type_id(loan_type_id: Optional[str]) -> bool:
    return bool(loan_type_id and loan_type_id in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS)

def _strip_optional_str(value: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]:
    if value is None:
        return None
    stripped = value.strip()
    return stripped if stripped else None


def _normalize_tags(v: List[str]) -> List[str]:
    return [t.strip().lower() for t in v if t and t.strip()]

class CreateSMBContactRequest(BaseModel):
    """
    Payload for creating a single SMB CRM contact.

    Required fields  : first_name, email
    Optional fields  : last_name, mobile, address, city, state, zip,
                       business_name, industry, monthly_revenue, time_in_business,
                       loan_type, tags
    """

    first_name: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=100)
    last_name: str = Field(default="", max_length=100)
    email: EmailStr
    mobile: str = Field(default="", max_length=30)

    address: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=255)
    city: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=100)
    state: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=100)
    zip: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=20)

    time_in_business: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=200)
    loan_type: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=50)

    tags: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

    @field_validator("first_name", "last_name")
    @classmethod
    def strip_name(cls, v: str) -> str:
        return v.strip()

    @field_validator(
        "address", "city", "state", "zip", "business_name", "industry",
        "monthly_revenue", "time_in_business",
    )
    @classmethod
    def strip_optional(cls, v: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]:
        return _strip_optional_str(v)

    @field_validator("loan_type")
    @classmethod
    def validate_loan_type(cls, v: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]:
        if v is None or not str(v).strip():
            return None
        resolved = resolve_loan_type(str(v))
        if not resolved:
            raise ValueError(
                "loan_type is not recognized. Choose a loan type from the CRM list."
            )
        return resolved

    @field_validator("tags")
    @classmethod
    def clean_tags(cls, v: List[str]) -> List[str]:
        return _normalize_tags(v)

def _resolve(row: dict[str, str], canonical: str) -> str:
    for alias in _ALIASES[canonical]:

    """Normalize lender-entered mobile for create/update; empty string clears."""
    raw = (mobile_raw or "").strip()

    normalized = try_normalize_mobile(raw)
    if not normalized:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,

                "message": (

            },

    return normalized

    *,
    row_num: int,
    email: str,
) -> tuple[str, list[CSVRowError]]:
    """Normalize a CSV mobile value; fall back to storing best-effort E.164."""
    warnings: list[CSVRowError] = []
    if not mobile_raw:
        warnings.append(CSVRowError(
            row=row_num,
            email=email,
            field="mobile",
            reason=(
                "Mobile number is missing. Contact was imported — "
                "add a phone number via Edit Contact before calling."
            ),
            severity="warning",
        ))
        return "", warnings

    normalized = try_normalize_mobile(mobile_raw)
    if normalized:
        return normalized, warnings

    warnings.append(CSVRowError(
        row=row_num,
        email=email,
        field="mobile",
        reason=(
            f"Could not verify phone number '{mobile_raw}'. "
            "Contact was imported without a phone number — "
            "fix it in Edit Contact before calling."
        ),
        severity="warning",
    ))
    return "", warnings

    raw: dict[str, str],

) -> tuple[Optional[str], list[CSVRowError]]:
    warnings: list[CSVRowError] = []
    loan_type_raw = _resolve(raw, "loan_type")
    if not loan_type_raw:
        return None, warnings
    loan_type_stored = resolve_loan_type(loan_type_raw)
    if loan_type_stored:
        return loan_type_stored, warnings
    warnings.append(CSVRowError(
        row=row_num,
        email=email,
        field="loan_type",
        reason=(
            f"Unrecognized loan type '{loan_type_raw}'. "
            "Contact was imported without a loan type — "
            "you can set it in Edit Contact."
        ),
        severity="warning",
    ))
    return None, warnings


def _apply_csv_row_to_contact(
    contact: SMBContact,
    raw: dict[str, str],
    *,
    row_num: int,
    email: str,
    identity_locked: bool,
) -> tuple[bool, list[CSVRowError]]:
    """Merge CSV row fields into an existing contact. Returns (changed, warnings)."""
    warnings: list[CSVRowError] = []
    changed = False

    first_name = _resolve(raw, "first_name")
    last_name = _resolve(raw, "last_name")
    if not identity_locked:
        if first_name and contact.first_name != first_name:
            contact.first_name = first_name
            changed = True
        new_last = last_name or ""
        if contact.last_name != new_last:
            contact.last_name = new_last
            changed = True
